description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with a robust, metallic construction and a dark, matte finish. The handles are slightly curved for ergonomic grip and appear to be made entirely of metal, lacking any visible rubber or plastic insulation. The jaws are flat and broad, designed for gripping and manipulating objects, with no visible serrations or wire-cutting features. The pivot mechanism is a simple, fixed joint, allowing the two arms to open and close smoothly. The overall design is utilitarian, suitable for general-purpose gripping tasks in urban environments such as garages or workshops. There are no visible brand markings or logos on the surface.
description: A sturdy, all-metal pair of pliers with curved handles and flat, broad jaws, finished in a dark matte color. The design is simple and utilitarian, with no visible insulation or branding.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the pliers, highlighting the curved handles and the flat, broad jaws.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the open jaws and the symmetrical curvature of the handles.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, emphasizing the uniformity of the handles and jaw alignment.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the back of the jaws and the wide opening between the handles.
attribute_handle_type: Curved, straight metal handles without insulation or ergonomic padding.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, broad jaws with no serrations or wire-cutting features; designed for general gripping.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal surface with no visible texturing or pattern.
